page: Amazon Web Services dl1.24xlarge is a deep learning server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores, utilizing Intel Xeon 8275CL processors running at 3.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For hardware acceleration, the instance includes eight Habana Gaudi HL-205 GPUs with a combined 256 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 4000 GB of NVMe SSD space, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the dl1.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a strong score of 94566.82, while the single-core performance of 1569.1 fell in the average range. Memory bandwidth tests showed strong results, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5249.26 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 98.61 GB/sec. Passmark CPU tests also returned strong scores, particularly in extended instructions and encryption. Database operations and Redis SET operations both registered strong performance, with Redis achieving 79.96 million operations per second.

The hardware profile of the dl1.24xlarge represents a high-density resource allocation, combining specialized Habana Gaudi accelerators with substantial system memory and local NVMe storage. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can leverage the specialized Gaudi architecture instead of general-purpose GPUs. However, a potential tradeoff is the average single-core performance observed in Geekbench and stress-ng tests, indicating that single-threaded tasks will not see the same acceleration as parallelized workloads. This instance is designed for deep learning training, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services dl1.24xlarge is a specialized deep learning server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 4000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The primary acceleration is delivered by eight Habana Gaudi HL-205 GPUs providing 256 GB of total VRAM. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database and Redis throughput, though single-core metrics remain in the average tier. This hardware density makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads. It is suited for deep learning model training, large-scale software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: An accelerator-dense platform featuring eight specialized GPUs, high-capacity NVMe storage, and strong multi-core processing for parallelized workloads.

96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 memory supporting high-concurrency tasks.
4000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Average single-core processing speeds relative to multi-threaded capabilities.
Best suited for deep learning training, database operations, and software compilation.
